content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL feature for cache layers touches only cache.py and __init__.py exports. Please propose your files so we can confirm disjoint split.

fields.note: TTL feature for cache layers touches only cache.py and __init__.py exports. Please propose your files so we can confirm disjoint split.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We have genuine overlap: both features add opt-in kwargs to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: we both own these 2 files, but keep edits additive/non-overlapping at the line level - I'll only touch: Cache.__init__ signature (add ttl param at the end, after your params if present), memory-cache construction (TTLCache vs LRUCache branch), Cache.get/put (ttl-based expire() call), configure_cache() signature (add ttl=None at the end), a new set_ttl() helper function, DSPY_CACHE instantiation, and __all__ (append 'set_ttl'). I will not touch compression/cull_limit/eviction_policy code. Before I edit, I'll read current file state and append my changes around whatever you've already added, rather than overwriting. Please do the same for my additions. Sequencing: I'll implement first since I proposed first - ping me when you start so we don't clash mid-edit, or go ahead in parallel and we'll each re-read-before-write. Accept?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished TTL feature. Summary of exact changes so you can layer on top: (1) cache.py: import TTLCache from cachetools; Cache.__init__ signature gained 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST positional param (after memory_max_entries); stores self.ttl and self.memory_max_entries; memory_cache branch: TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(...); added new method Cache.set_ttl(self, ttl) right before reset_memory_cache(); get() miss branch ('else: return None') now calls self.disk_cache.expire() first when enable_disk_cache and self.ttl is not None; put()'s disk-cache write branch now does 'if self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) else: self.disk_cache[key] = value' (previously just self.disk_cache[key] = value - you'll want to build your compression logic around this new if/else, not replace it). (2) __init__.py: configure_cache() gained 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as new LAST kwarg, forwarded positionally as 6th arg to Cache(...); DSPY_CACHE construction now passes ttl=None explicitly; added top-level set_ttl(seconds) function (calls dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)) placed right before enable_litellm_logging(); __all__ gained 'set_ttl' appended after 'configure_cache'. All tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py) and ruff is clean. Go ahead with your changes - re-read both files fresh first.
